product_model.identity: SITE-AGNOSTIC autonomous data bridge. Coach picks prior service in TGP mobile → browser opens that site → after coach login, extension prompts Start client Import? → extension autonomously flips through many pages capturing JSON → posts to TGP scout ingest. Extension ships with the app by default.
product_model.not_this: Not a thin v0.1 TrueCoach-only stub. Not per-site hard-mapped tooling as the product core. Not server-side credential scraping.
product_model.launch_bar: v0.3 — full autonomous multi-page import loop. No half-launch.
flow: 1. User downloads TGP (extension included by default with the app)
4. Opens that site in browser; coach logs in with their own session
5. TGP extension popup: Start client Import?
6. Autonomous multi-page crawl — extension flips through pages and captures JSON as it loads
7. Envelopes to /api/scout/ingest; progress to mobile; complete settles import

product_mission_correction_2026_07_15.op_label: Op 54 — canonical product-mission correction under R5
product_mission_correction_2026_07_15.operator_quote_verbatim: WRONG - your building a site agnsotics, ultra easy to use, browser agnostic tool that can seamlessly and autonymously pull ALL data from any comeptitors site - send to TGP Database, and be reconstructed to our set values instantly with a luxury UI while doing so!
product_mission_correction_2026_07_15.quote_handling: Preserved byte-for-byte (including original spelling) at the top of the durable record per R5. Do NOT paraphrase or 'correct' the quote.
product_mission_correction_2026_07_15.what_it_fixes: Prior canonical wording (chiefly roadmap/M-IMPORTER-EXTENSION_v1.md as originally written) mistook TrueCoach v0.3 for the overall product and implied Chrome-only + a per-competitor mapped-extractor treadmill. That framing is WRONG. TrueCoach on Chrome is the FIRST proving adapter / vertical slice only.
product_mission_correction_2026_07_15.mission: Site-agnostic, browser-agnostic acquisition; autonomous learning/blueprint induction across competitor sites; extraction of ALL user-authorized accessible data; completeness/accounting so omissions are explicit; normalization into one canonical TGP import envelope; deterministic reconstruction into TGP's defined entities/values; idempotent replay; honest progress/partial/failure reporting; and an ultra-easy restrained-luxury UI.
legal_security_invariants: No credential storage on TGP servers
No bypass of source access controls (safe methods only; ride the session, never break it)
Fail closed on ambiguous mappings (never guess a value)
Never claim inaccessible data was imported (honest accounting by construction)
Source bearer in-memory single-run only; never DOM/telemetry/storage/logs/PR/payload
SSRF confinement via allowedOrigins normalized before any fetch

five_distinctions.mission: North star (site-agnostic + browser-agnostic + autonomous + deterministic reconstruction + luxury UI). Never a single PR.
five_distinctions.first_proof: TrueCoach on a Chrome MV3 host — one site + one browser host proving the whole pipeline end-to-end. A vertical slice, not the product boundary.
five_distinctions.current_state: The live PRs in this snapshot (EXT-C1b PR #6 @55f24d5 merge-ready; Mobile M2 PR #285 @10414c4 dual-lens r2 in progress) — unchanged and authoritative.
five_distinctions.v0_3_completion: Full autonomous multi-page import loop for the first proof, default-flagged, honestly reported, packaged, staging E2E green, R137 postmortem. Does NOT require a 2nd site, 2nd browser, or auto-induction. Current launch bar ('v0.3 or no launch').
five_distinctions.v1_0_acceptance: Mission made testable: >=3 sites, >=2 browser hosts on the same kernel, autonomous blueprint induction for an unseen site, completeness/reconciliation accounting, deterministic + idempotent reconstruction, honest partial/failure reporting, luxury UI passes R1 crit, all §2 invariants hold under adversarial audit.

WS4 canonical mapping/reconstruction (backend reconstruct* + idempotent upsert + reconciliation report)
WS6 luxury UI (mobile import/pairing/progress/review screens; extension popup HTML/CSS only)

r3_process_incidents.nature: UNLIKE R3-INC-1 and R3-INC-2 (which were ACCIDENTAL process failures where the identity-safe path was simply not followed), R3-INC-3 is a DELIBERATE, EXPLICITLY OPERATOR-AUTHORIZED one-time bypass of the R3 identity requirement and the R3_MERGE_RUNBOOK git-native path. It is an exception TO the canonical rule, NOT a change OF it, and is NOT to be treated as a new default.
r3_process_incidents.summary: Mobile PR-M3 #287 (audited head 95c9aea1b2905440a37cb9762e4486e45747165c, base b8165beaa3804fe8a145214b772f97a3ae9eab65) was landed on mobile main via `gh pr merge 287 --squash --delete-branch` - the GitHub server-side squash path that the R3_MERGE_RUNBOOK explicitly FORBIDS for production main (section 1.1). The resulting merge commit e3a824f335ef75934fe860165ffc9c41a7b7956b is NOT R3-compliant: author BradleyGleavePortfolio <bradleyapple1031@gmail.com>, committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> (GitHub login web-flow), and the squash body carries a `Co-authored-by: Bradley Gleave <bradley@bradleytgpcoaching.com>` trailer. R3 requires author AND committer to be Bradley Gleave <bradley@bradleytgpcoaching.com> with no co-author tokens; none of those three conditions is met.
r3_process_incidents.why_bypass_happened: The merge executors REFUSED to force author+committer to Bradley Gleave, explicitly declining it as provenance impersonation. The human operator then OVERRODE with an explicit one-time authorization.
r3_process_incidents.operator_authorization_verbatim: just merge it under whatever name - a one time bypass
r3_process_incidents.authorization_scope: ONE-TIME bypass for landing mobile PR #287 only. It does NOT authorize any future server-side merge, does NOT change R3, does NOT change the R3_MERGE_RUNBOOK, and does NOT retroactively make e3a824f R3-clean. The runbook git-native path remains the default and sole standing mechanism for production main on all TGP repos.

merge_procedure_change_2026_07_14.trigger: R3-INC-1 — GitHub-generated squash stamps a non-Bradley author/committer identity that R3 forbids, and correcting it after publish would require a destructive force-push over shared main.
merge_procedure_change_2026_07_14.old_procedure: Agent used GitHub squash-merge (UI/API) after dual-lens CLEAN + CI green (R138).
merge_procedure_change_2026_07_14.new_procedure: Do NOT use GitHub-generated squash for future TGP merges. Instead: (1) build a local/manual squash commit whose tree is exactly the audited exact-head tree, with BOTH author and committer set to Bradley Gleave <bradley@bradleytgpcoaching.com>; (2) lease-safe fast-forward main to that commit (--force-with-lease pinned to the known base, aborts on divergence — fast-forward only, no history rewrite); (3) close the PR referencing the landed commit.
merge_procedure_change_2026_07_14.scope: Applies to extension, backend, and mobile TGP repos going forward. Prior grandfathered history (including 5eabeec) is NOT rewritten.
merge_procedure_change_2026_07_14.supersedes_note: Refines R138 autonomy: merge remains agent-permitted after dual-lens CLEAN + CI green, but the mechanism changes from GitHub squash to identity-safe manual squash + lease-safe fast-forward.
merge_procedure_change_2026_07_14.known_violation: R3-INC-2 (backend PR #509, 2026-07-16): this procedure was NOT followed for #509 — it was landed via GitHub-generated squash, producing non-Bradley identity on merge commit 1718293 (recorded honestly; NOT rewritten, NOT claimed R3-clean).
merge_procedure_change_2026_07_14.op58_remediation: REMEDIATED / PROVEN FORWARD (Op 58, 2026-07-16): the git-native squash + PLAIN fast-forward path is now codified as the SOLE mandated mechanism for landing on any production main in handoffs/importer-wave/R3_MERGE_RUNBOOK.md, empirically proven by backend PR #508 / 95e2c63 (author AND committer both Bradley Gleave). This Op 58 runbook SUPERSEDES the '--force-with-lease' mechanism named in new_procedure above: on main we use a PLAIN fast-forward push with NO --force-with-lease and NO force of any kind (git's own non-fast-forward rejection is the drift guard). --force-with-lease is used NOWHERE on main (it remains permitted only for wip/* snapshot branches). Server-side squash / gh pr merge / REST-GraphQL merge endpoints / web edit-commit are FORBIDDEN for main. R3-INC-2 hard gate is CLEARED as the merge-path blocker; reversible importer work has reopened; IMPORTER-F remains blocked ONLY on D2.

audit_rounds.lens_b_findings: Independent read-only lens; did NOT read Lens A. P3s: (1) partial-reported-as-succeeded — handleStartImport collapses result.status complete||partial both to ingest_succeeded, discarding the engine's honest partial/degraded/truncated signal (silent data loss shown as unqualified success); (2) tgp-authloss-status-muddle — makeSender throws plain Error('auth_required') not AuthLostError, so a TGP-side auth loss lands in the catch as detail 'auth_required' and overwrites the friendlier onAuthLost broadcast in an order-dependent way; (3) single-flight-only-start_import — importInFlight guards start_import only, not the legacy start_ingest path, so start_ingest can run concurrently. decision_record_op58_reconcile_2026_07_1.decision: Codify the git-native squash + PLAIN fast-forward merge path as the SOLE mandated mechanism for landing importer-wave PRs on any production main, via a new canonical runbook (handoffs/importer-wave/R3_MERGE_RUNBOOK.md). Permanently FORBID server-side merges for production main: the GitHub squash UI button, gh pr merge (any mode), the REST/GraphQL merge endpoints, and GitHub web edit/commit. Mark R3-INC-2 remediation PROVEN FORWARD and the incident CONTAINED; grandfather the historical merge commit 1718293 (NOT rewritten, NOT claimed compliant). Reopen reversible importer work; keep D2 OPEN/PROTECTED and IMPORTER-F blocked on D2. Do NOT decide D2; do NOT touch backend/mobile product code; do NOT edit AGENT_RULES verbatim or relax R3/R14/R15/R102.
decision_record_op58_reconcile_2026_07_1.why: R3-INC-1 and R3-INC-2 are the same failure class: GitHub's server-side squash cannot set author AND committer to Bradley Gleave <bradley@bradleytgpcoaching.com> (it forces committer=GitHub and author=account identity). The defect is AVOIDABLE, not inherent — backend PR #508 landed 95e2c6378e0b1b734328a7fdf6b9a6e33465a663 on backend main with full R3 author AND committer via a git-native squash-and-push one commit earlier. A durable process fix (forbid the re-authoring path; mandate the proven git-native path with fail-safe commands) closes the recurrence without any destructive force-push of shared main.
decision_record_op58_reconcile_2026_07_1.empirical_proof: backend PR #508 -> 95e2c6378e0b1b734328a7fdf6b9a6e33465a663 (author AND committer both Bradley Gleave <bradley@bradleytgpcoaching.com>, git-native squash-and-push).
decision_record_op58_reconcile_2026_07_1.mandated_path_summary: (1) verify exact audited head + base with R124 both-ways and confirm base == live remote main tip (drift guard); (2) git commit-tree with tree == audited-head tree, single parent == pinned base, BOTH author AND committer forced to Bradley Gleave via env + -c; (3) MANDATORY preflight identity check (author, committer, tree, parent) before any push; (4) PLAIN fast-forward push (git push origin <sha>:main) with NO --force, NO --force-with-lease ANYWHERE on main, NO admin bypass — git's non-fast-forward rejection is the drift guard; (5) MANDATORY post-push verification of remote identity + tree + required checks; STOP if branch protection rejects (no unprotect, no bypass).

decision_record_op59_reconcile_2026_07_1.decision: D2 DECIDED: imported clients are invite-pending, non-login tenant-owned canonical Person/roster records (hardened Option 1). No AuthPrincipal or credential is created during import; a Person is linked to an AuthPrincipal ONLY after an explicit credential-verified claim and verified ownership, through a unique AccountLink. Email is unverified imported data - never a canonical id and never an automatic linking key. Opaque server-issued person_id; tenant-scoped external_ref {source_platform, source_person_id} as idempotency/dedup key; states InvitePending -> Invited -> Claimed -> Suspended -> Deleted; single-use, single-tenant, short-lived (<=5 min), unguessable claim tokens; atomic claim (AuthPrincipal + AccountLink + status->Claimed in one transaction or roll back all three); rollback and erasure cascade imported data and links. Options 2 (create a full auth User at import) and 3 (separate staging identity as source of truth) are REJECTED. Billing/payment credentials/methods/cards/vault tokens/profiles/subscription instruments remain an explicit excluded family.
decision_record_op59_reconcile_2026_07_1.authorization: Explicit operator directive this session: research cybersecurity and RLS, select the top D2 option, and go with it. Closes the previously OPEN/PROTECTED D2 node.
decision_record_op59_reconcile_2026_07_1.real_goal: Immediate, deterministic, coach-visible import continuity with zero premature credentials and one canonical source of truth, while keeping tenant isolation, consent, deletion, and auditability intact.
decision_record_op59_reconcile_2026_07_1.root_cause: The placement choice fixes the authorization key (tenant vs subject uid), the dedup/idempotency key, the linking gate, and the deletion cascade - none of which can be designed until the record’s relationship to a credential is fixed. That is why D2 blocked IMPORTER-F.

options.option_1_selected: Invite-pending non-login roster shell (hardened) - one tenant-owned Person, no credential; deterministic credential-verified link on claim. SELECTED.
options.option_2_rejected: Create a full auth User at import - phantom credentialed accounts keyed on unverified imported email (account-takeover / auto-email-linking pattern), email-uniqueness collisions, unverified recovery paths, high blast radius. REJECTED.
options.option_3_rejected: Separate staging identity as source of truth - two live PII stores, drift, multiplied tenant-isolation and deletion-failure surface, no offsetting benefit at TGP scale. REJECTED.

decision_record_op59_reconcile_2026_07_1.five_step: Questioned the false “login at import” requirement (records exist without credentials - Entra/SCIM); Deleted premature credentials/temp passwords and any second identity store; Simplified to one Person entity + a status flag (SCIM active pattern); Accelerated with instant deterministic import (verification deferred to claim); Automated claim/link/verification last (automatic email-linking explicitly NOT automated).
decision_record_op59_reconcile_2026_07_1.idiot_index: Option 1 cost ~= intrinsic cost of one person row; Options 2/3 pay full auth-principal / second-store overhead for the same contact - high idiot index for no functional gain.
decision_record_op59_reconcile_2026_07_1.extreme_test: 10x (10k clients/one import): O(1) per row via opaque id + external_ref upsert; Option 2 would provision 10k credential-less logins. 100x (same human across coaches): correctly two tenant-scoped Person rows (NIST single-subscriber binding); Option 2 email-keyed logins collide (AliasExistsException) and can leak across tenants. Worst case (hostile competitor export): no credentials minted, so a poisoned import can at most create quarantined non-login roster rows in one tenant, contained by fail-closed RLS and reversible via rollback/erasure.
decision_record_op59_reconcile_2026_07_1.hyperscaler: Copy (evidence-backed): credential-less pending record (Entra), prestaged profile linked before first sign-in (Cognito AdminLinkProviderForUser), stable opaque id + externalId dedup + active status + write-only password (SCIM 7643/7644), record-then-bind + no-email-identifiers + single-use short-lived link tokens (NIST 800-63C-4), fail-closed RLS + no exposed service key (Supabase), deny-by-default + opaque ids + every-request object checks (OWASP). Do NOT copy: full IdP/federation stack, PPIs/FAL2 machinery, SCIM bulk protocol, temp-password issuance, enterprise directory ops.
decision_record_op59_reconcile_2026_07_1.rls_intent: Deny-by-default / fail-closed; RLS enabled on every exposed table with nothing granted until a policy allows it. Pre-claim roster rows are tenant/coach-scoped (no end-user auth.uid() yet - a subject-uid predicate would silently fail). Post-claim rows are subject-uid + tenant scoped. Importer runs a server-only controlled path; RLS-bypassing service/bypassrls credentials are never exposed to the browser or customers. Every request gets an object-level tenant check; ids are opaque and non-guessable.
decision_record_op59_reconcile_2026_07_1.good_without_bad: Keep deterministic import, roster continuity, no premature credential, single source of truth, credentialed explicit claim + deterministic link, tenant isolation + fail-closed authz, idempotent replay, rollback, audit, deletion. Exclude duplicate staging truth, automatic email-only linking, phantom logins, cross-tenant leakage, billing ingestion, and heavy enterprise identity infra.
decision_record_op59_reconcile_2026_07_1.evidence: Primary-source research report (fetched this session, every claim inline-cited): /home/user/workspace/D2_universal_importer_identity_decision_report.md. Sources: Microsoft Entra External ID (learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/external-id/user-properties); AWS Cognito federated linking (docs.aws.amazon.com/cognito/latest/developerguide/cognito-user-pools-identity-federation-consolidate-users.html), email alias uniqueness (.../user-pool-settings-attributes.html), sign-up/admin states (.../signing-up-users-in-your-app.html); SCIM RFC 7643 (rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7643) + RFC 7644 (rfc-editor.org/rfc/pdfrfc/rfc7644.txt.pdf); NIST SP 800-63-4 (csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/63/4/final) + 800-63C-4 (pages.nist.gov/800-63-4/sp800-63c.html); Auth0 account linking concept + implementation (auth0.com/docs/manage-users/user-accounts/user-account-linking[/link-user-accounts]); Firebase account linking (firebase.google.com/docs/auth/web/account-linking); Supabase RLS (supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security); OWASP Authorization Cheat Sheet (cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Authorization_Cheat_Sheet.html); GDPR Art.5 (gdpr-info.eu/art-5-gdpr/) + Art.17 (gdpr-info.eu/art-17-gdpr/).
decision_record_op59_reconcile_2026_07_1.rollback_stop: If claim volume or fraud shows the manual invite->claim path cannot scale, revisit AUTOMATION of claim (step 5) - never revert to auto-creating logins. Stop condition: any design that mints a credential before verified ownership, or uses email as a canonical/linking key. Docs/state only - reversible by reverting this commit; no runtime/flag/data impact.

r138_gate.musk_5: Questioned the false login-at-import requirement; deleted premature credentials + any second store; simplified to one Person + status; accelerated with instant deterministic import; automated claim last. A phantom credentialed account should not exist.
r138_gate.hyperscalers: Entra, Cognito, SCIM, NIST 800-63C, Auth0, Firebase, Supabase, OWASP all separate a record/profile from an auth principal and gate linking on verified ownership + credential proof (copied); heavy federation machinery not copied.
r138_gate.good_without_bad: Roster continuity + instant import without phantom logins, auto-email-linking, cross-tenant leakage, or a duplicate store - gated by credential-verified atomic claim, opaque ids, and fail-closed tenant RLS.
r138_gate.root_cause: Fixes the record<->credential relationship (record exists credential-free; credential bound only on verified claim), unblocking the authorization key, dedup key, linking gate, and deletion cascade IMPORTER-F needs.

decision_record_op68_v5_stack_lock_2026_.title: V5 STACK LOCK + no_truecoach_mapper CORRECTION - the last leg of the TrueCoach vertical proof (multi-adapter, site-agnostic-core proof) is defined into a smallest-canonical, dependency-ordered PR stack with a core-diff-zero pass gate. decision_record_op68_v5_stack_lock_2026_.decision: Lock the V5 stack + order: PR-1 (backend) thin source_platform->mapper registry, TrueCoach-only, BEHAVIOR-IDENTICAL (replace the hard-wired families.ts dispatch with a registry keyed by source_platform; register only 'truecoach' wrapping the existing mapTrueCoachClient/mapTrueCoachEntity unchanged; NO behavior/family/flag/contract/DTO/schema/migration change; contract stays 1.4.0 byte-identical per R80) -> PR-2a (backend) conformance_alpha mapper + deterministic golden fixture + conformance/core-diff-zero test  PARALLEL WITH  PR-2b (extension) data-only conformance_alpha blueprint + recorded CDP fixture + blueprint/e2e specs (engine untouched); parallel is safe because 2a/2b share only the frozen source_platform token + fixture descriptor -> PR-3 (cross-repo) deterministic end-to-end proof driving BOTH adapters through the same unchanged core -> PR-4 (context) reconcile recording PASS/FAIL of the two-adapter/no-core-change gate. Mobile = NO-OP unless PR-3 exposes a real source-neutral contract gap. Adapter #2 = 'conformance_alpha' (exact shared token both repos), explicitly non-production (never coach-selectable, never in a production platform list, exercised only by deterministic fixtures), structurally independent of TrueCoach across endpoint topology / identifier format / nesting / pagination / optional-missing fields / poison-row behavior, but inside the existing extension boundary (REST, GET/HEAD, page|cursor, single itemsPath/idField, JWT/cookie) so NO engine change is required. A single versioned conformance_alpha.fixture.json descriptor (fixture_schema_version 1.0.0) is the source of truth for BOTH surfaces so backend + extension cannot diverge.
decision_record_op68_v5_stack_lock_2026_.governing_decision: Op 63 'Defer messaging' (decision_record_op63_v0_defer_messaging_2026_07_19 + DECISION_LOG Op-63) - the R138 four-question directional gate for the whole vertical-proof scope, which already planned V5 as the last leg. Op 68 tightens the DEFINITION of that leg and records verified product-repo facts; it is NOT a new directional decision, so no fresh R138 gate is re-run. A fresh R138 gate IS required before any new migration/table/flag/queue/workflow inside the V5 build.

decision_record_op68_v5_stack_lock_2026_.core_diff_zero_gate: Enforced AFTER PR-1 lands. Adapter #2 (PR-2a + PR-2b) may add ONLY: the conformance_alpha mapper, the data-only blueprint, config, fixtures, tests, and EXACTLY ONE source_platform->mapper registry registration line. FORBIDDEN (any one = V5 NOT passed -> STOP, redesign into adapter-local mapping): any change to the replay engine (runReplay), any DTO, the OpenAPI schema/contract (must stay 1.4.0 byte-identical per R80), any migration, any RLS/storage change, any mobile UI change, any new flag/table/queue/workflow, any 'if source == ...' branch in the core. PROOF OF PASS: (a) git diff over the core path-set (engine, DTOs, OpenAPI, migrations, RLS, mobile UI) is EMPTY except the single registry line; (b) grep of the core (excluding the registry file + adapter-local dirs) for 'conformance_alpha' returns 0; (c) both adapters' conformance suites green on recorded fixtures.
decision_record_op68_v5_stack_lock_2026_.cross_run_resume_deferred: The extension engine has in-memory idempotency ONLY; NO cross-run / process-restart checkpoint. V5 does NOT prove crash-safe cross-run resume. V5's idempotency claim is limited to (a) within-run replay convergence and (b) backend external_ref dedupe on re-ingest. Evidence trigger: only if a fault-injection drill requires resuming after a process kill/restart. If wanted, it is a SEPARATE pre-build-reviewed slice (candidate PR-C-RESUME) with its own R14 cycle. Do NOT claim V5 proves cross-run resume until that slice lands.

next_frontier_v5.framing: V5 is a MULTI-ADAPTER end-to-end proof, NOT a TrueCoach-first dogfood phase. TrueCoach is one interchangeable validation adapter and confers no privilege (R-SITE-AGNOSTIC-1). The point of V5 is to PROVE the reconstruct core is genuinely site-agnostic by exercising it end-to-end through MORE THAN ONE structurally independent adapter.
hard_requirements: REQUIRE at least TWO structurally independent adapters (distinct source schema/shape, not a config variant of the same one) driven end-to-end through the same reconstruct core (capture/ingest -> multi-family reconstruct -> read contract 1.4.0 -> mobile counts/reasons UX).

NO core changes are permitted to land the SECOND adapter: if adding adapter #2 forces edits to the shared reconstruct core (services, DTOs, contract, RLS, storage), the core is not yet site-agnostic and V5 has NOT passed - the fix is to push the difference into adapter-local mapping, not the core.
Adapter-local mapping only: each adapter contributes its own source->canonical mapper; the core stays adapter-agnostic. No adapter-specific branches in the core.

End-to-end on deterministic golden fixtures per adapter first; real-account validation is exercised in V5 (was deferred to V5 by Op 63) but the two-adapter/no-core-change gate is the pass condition, not any single real account.
All feature flags stay default-off outside the staging exercise; billing remains excluded; messaging remains deferred.
stop_conditions: STOP if only one adapter is wired (single-adapter dogfood is NOT V5).
STOP if landing the second adapter requires ANY change to the shared reconstruct core - re-scope the difference into adapter-local mapping before proceeding.
STOP if a second progress system, totals/percentage/ETA surface, Deleted/tombstone erasure state, adapter-specific core contract, or billing capture is introduced (hard-constraint DELETE list holds).
STOP and gate under R138 before any new migration/table/flag/queue/workflow.
STOP if V5 work attempts to fold in the separate backend automation failures (build-sbom/release-please) - those stay in their own infra lane.

No new migration/table/flag/queue/workflow/totals-scan/cross-family-join/source-DTO/claim-credential/billing/messaging/second-progress/Deleted-state was built by PR-M4 (the hard-constraint DELETE list held). automation_failure_disposition.release_please: failure: 'release-please failed: GitHub Actions is not permitted to create or approve pull requests.' A repo/org GitHub Actions permission setting (Settings -> Actions -> 'Allow GitHub Actions to create and approve pull requests'), not a code condition. Pre-existing / diff-independent: also failure on prior main f9b81cf.

decision_record_op63_v0_defer_messaging_.title: V0 SCOPE DECISION - DEFER MESSAGING: the TrueCoach end-to-end vertical proof (IMPORT-VERT) v1 covers CLIENTS + WORKOUTS + CLIENT HISTORY; messaging becomes a later specialized lane, NOT a generic entity family in v1
decision_record_op63_v0_defer_messaging_.kind: R138-governed directional/scope decision (docs/state only; no product code, no AGENT_RULES change, no runbook change)
decision_record_op63_v0_defer_messaging_.decision: Per explicit operator ruling ('Defer messaging'), the v1 TrueCoach end-to-end vertical proof covers three data families - CLIENTS, WORKOUTS, and CLIENT HISTORY - all of which are already captured and staged today. Messaging is DEFERRED to a later, specialized lane (its own extractor/reconstruction contract) rather than being built as a generic entity family inside the v1 proof. Consequent scope resolutions, all operator-directed: (1) NEXT STEP is BACKEND-FIRST = IMPORTER-H (backend multi-family reconstruct: parametrize the clients-only reconstruct/roster path to also reconstruct workouts + client history into canonical TGP entities under the D2 identity model); because no new capture is required for these families, no extension lane precedes it. (2) Build/audit runs against DETERMINISTIC golden fixtures; REAL-ACCOUNT end-to-end validation is DEFERRED to V5 (the staging full-loop dogfood). (3) Mobile review UX for the proof is the MINIMAL honest per-family counts/reasons screen (staged=reconstructed+skipped+failed with reasons; no invented completion/percentage), NOT the full diff/suggested-fixes luxury surface (deferred product enhancement). (4) Autonomous site learning / blueprint induction is a SEPARATE follow-on lane, not part of this v1 proof. (5) Branch controls on product mains are UNCHANGED (git-native non-fast-forward drift guard remains the control; enabling server-side protection is a separate operator-reconcile item, not part of this decision). (6) NO permanent R3 change: R3 and the R3_MERGE_RUNBOOK remain the sole default doctrine; every V-PR lands via the git-native path; the standing executor-identity tension is resolved separately before the first V-PR land (unchanged from Op 62). This is a scope-narrowing decision that de-risks the proof and removes the longest pole (greenfield messaging) from the critical path.

operator_authorization.scope: v1 of the TrueCoach vertical proof only. Messaging remains an in-scope product data family for the mission (workouts, client history, messaging - billing excluded); it is deferred from v1 to a later specialized lane, NOT dropped from the product. Does not alter the D2 model, the billing exclusion, the immutable landed build order, or any protected rule.
operator_authorization.reason: Messaging is greenfield across all three repos (no extension extractor, no messages bucket, nothing downstream) - the longest pole and the driver of an extension-first order. Clients/workouts/client-history are already captured and staged, so deferring messaging lets the proof go backend-first (IMPORTER-H) and prove the whole pipeline faster on data that already exists.

deferred.blueprint_induction: separate follow-on lane (autonomous site learning), not part of v1 proof
deferred.review_ux_luxury: full diff-by-family + suggested fixes + single commit deferred; v1 ships minimal honest counts/reasons
deferred.real_account_validation: deferred to V5 staging full-loop dogfood; build/audit uses deterministic golden fixtures

v1_proof_scope.billing: EXCLUDED (unchanged) - never captured/staged/reconstructed; accounted 'excluded', never skipped/failed.

Parametrize the hardcoded RECONSTRUCT_ENTITY_TYPE='clients' path to reconstruct workouts + client history into canonical TGP entities under D2 (invite-pending Person/roster identity model; tenant-scoped external_ref idempotency; email never a linking key; fail-closed RLS). Deterministic golden fixtures for workouts + client history; honest staged=reconstructed+skipped+failed accounting per family; default-off behind FEATURE_SCOUT_INGEST + FEATURE_SCOUT_RECONSTRUCT (no new flags). Then IMPORTER-I (mobile-readable per-family review read contract), then PR-M4 (mobile minimal honest counts/reasons review screen), then V5 (staging full-loop dogfood incl. real-account validation). NOT dispatched this Op - awaits the R14 audit cycle on the IMPORTER-H build PR.

r138_decision_gate.q1_first_principles_musk_algorithm: QUESTION every requirement: 'the proof must include all mission families at once' has no owner-attached necessity - the proof's job is to de-risk the pipeline, which three already-captured families do. DELETE the part: cut greenfield messaging (and the generic-messaging-entity build) from v1 - the biggest deletion, removing the longest pole; also delete blueprint induction and luxury review UX from v1. SIMPLIFY what survives: reuse the existing clients-only reconstruct/roster path, parametrized for workouts+history (one path, not per-family engines). ACCELERATE: deterministic golden fixtures give fast, repeatable build/audit; real-account validation batched to V5. AUTOMATE last: no new automation/flags introduced. Musk's warning applied: do not optimize (build a generic messaging entity) a thing that should not exist in v1.
r138_decision_gate.q2_hyperscaler_lens_evidence: What would hyperscalers do: ship the thinnest vertical slice that exercises the whole pipeline, behind flags, validated on deterministic fixtures before real data - the AWS/GCP progressive-delivery + canary pattern (small blast radius first, widen later). Deferring real-account validation to a staging dogfood (V5) mirrors canary/one-box rollout before production; keeping every family behind the existing default-off scout flags is blast-radius containment via feature gating rather than added human latency (AWS Builders' Library continuous delivery; Google Cloud safe rollouts). Multi-family reconstruct as a parametrized path over one contract is the platform pattern of one generalized pipeline over per-type forks.
r138_decision_gate.q3_good_without_bad: GOOD unlocked: a faster, lower-risk end-to-end proof on data that already exists, proving capture -> stage -> reconstruct -> honest review for real families. BAD risked: (a) messaging looking 'done' when deferred - MITIGATED by recording it as an explicit deferred specialized lane (honest accounting; truth_boundaries.no_e2e_proof_yet still holds); (b) a clients-only shortcut masquerading as multi-family - MITIGATED by IMPORTER-H genuinely reconstructing workouts+history with per-family staged=reconstructed+skipped+failed accounting (R109 'No Half-Ass'); (c) fixture-only proof mistaken for real-world validation - MITIGATED by explicitly deferring real-account validation to V5 and not claiming e2e completion. Structure that keeps GOOD, gates BAD: default-off flags + deterministic fixtures + honest per-family accounting + dual-lens R14 audit on each V-PR + git-native R3 land.
r138_decision_gate.q4_root_cause: Attacks the root cause: the proof was blocked on an undecided scope (messaging greenfield forced extension-first and a longer path). Fixing the scope (defer messaging, go backend-first with families that exist) removes the actual blocker rather than papering over it. It does not work around any quality gate - the R14 audit, R3 identity, D2 identity model, billing exclusion, and default-off posture are all preserved.

risks_mitigations: Risk: 'multi-family' reconstruct becomes a thin clients-clone. Mitigation: IMPORTER-H must reconstruct workouts + client history into real canonical entities with per-family accounting and byte-pinned golden fixtures; dual-lens audit verifies non-triviality (R109).

Risk: deferring messaging is read as descoping it from the product. Mitigation: recorded as a DEFERRAL to a later specialized lane, not a drop; mission (workouts/client history/messaging, billing excluded) unchanged.

Risk: deterministic-fixture proof over-claims real-world readiness. Mitigation: real-account validation explicitly deferred to V5; truth_boundaries.no_e2e_proof_yet unchanged; no e2e completion claimed.

Risk: executor-identity tension (R3-INC-3) recurs on the first V-PR land. Mitigation: unchanged from Op 62 - resolve the signing-identity/runbook question before the first product-main land; no permanent R3 change made here.
Risk: scope creep back into v1 (luxury UX, blueprint induction). Mitigation: both explicitly recorded as separate follow-ons; R18 lane discipline keeps each V-PR single-concern.
rejected_alternatives: Include messaging in v1 (extension-first EXT-MSG capture then downstream): REJECTED - greenfield across all three repos, longest pole, delays proving the pipeline on data that already exists; operator ruled defer.

Build messaging as a generic entity family inside the v1 proof: REJECTED - messaging has a distinct capture/reconstruction shape (threaded, participant-scoped) that warrants its own specialized lane rather than being forced into the generic clients/workouts reconstruct path.
Full luxury review UX (diff-by-family + suggested fixes + single commit) in v1: REJECTED for v1 - minimal honest counts/reasons proves the review leg; luxury surface is a later product enhancement.
Bundle autonomous blueprint induction into this proof lane: REJECTED - separate follow-on; bundling would blow the lane scope and LOC caps.
Real-account end-to-end validation as a build/audit gate for IMPORTER-H: REJECTED for the build lane - deterministic fixtures gate build/audit; real-account validation batched to V5 staging dogfood.
Amend R3 / the runbook to ease the first V-PR land: REJECTED - no permanent R3 change; the git-native path remains mandatory; identity tension resolved separately.
